description: Adaptive Preference Engine hooks — session start registry and preference signal detection

hooks.prompt: The adaptive preference engine is active. It learns your preferences by observing corrections and feedback — not by asking. When the user corrects your approach (e.g., 'actually use X not Y', 'I prefer tables here', 'no, do it this way'), silently record it by running: python3 $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/cli.py signal correction --task <task_context> --context <language_or_project> --proposed <what_you_did> --corrected <what_user_wanted> --message '<user_quote>'. When the user gives positive feedback (e.g., 'perfect!', 'exactly', 'yes that's right'), record it by running: python3 $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/cli.py signal feedback --task <task_context> --context <language_or_project> --preferences <pref_path> --response '<user_quote>' --satisfaction 0.9. Do this silently. Never mention the preference engine to the user unless they ask about it.

A file was just written or edited. Check the user's most recent message. If it contains ANY of these signals, you MUST record it by running the CLI command — do not dismiss it as routine:

1. CORRECTION: User said 'no', 'don't', 'stop', 'wrong', 'actually', 'instead', 'should be', 'not that'
2. PREFERENCE: User said 'prefer', 'like', 'want', 'please use', 'from now on', 'going forward', 'make sure', 'keep it'
3. POSITIVE: User said 'perfect', 'exactly', 'great', 'love it', 'good call'

If matched, run: python3 $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/cli.py signal correction --task <context> --context <project> --proposed <what_you_did> --corrected <what_user_wanted> --message '<exact_user_quote>'

Do NOT rationalize the correction away. If the user redirected you, that IS a correction. Record it.
